pim.title: Product Information Management
pim.subtitle: Master product, variant, attribute, and category catalog.

products.title: Products
products.noProducts: No products in this workspace yet.
products.notFound: Product not found in this workspace.

variants.title: Variants
variants.noVariants: No variants for this product yet.

categories.title: Categories
categories.noCategories: No categories in this workspace yet.

attributeSets.title: Attribute sets
attributeSets.noSets: No attribute sets in this workspace yet.

automation.title: Automation
automation.subtitle: Live controller state for the vessels in this workspace. (Scheduling, capacity, and utilization belong to the future capacity-resource-planning module — not this surface.)
automation.noVessels: No vessels in this workspace yet. Vessels appear here once the automation supervisor receives the first snapshot from a connected adapter.
automation.notFound: Vessel not found in this workspace.

mrp.title: Production planning
mrp.subtitle: Read-only MRP alpha view over existing brewery recipes and brew sessions.
mrp.alphaNote: Rows marked as projected are read models. Brewery remains the source of truth; no MRP rows are created from this page.
mrp.error: Could not load production planning data.
mrp.noProductionOrders: No production orders or brewery projections in this workspace yet.
mrp.noMaterialRequirements: No material requirements are visible yet.

materialRequirements.title: Material requirements
materialRequirements.subtitle: Read-only entry point for per-order material requirements. Open an order to see its ingredient-derived requirements.

crp.title: Capacity planning
crp.subtitle: Read-only CRP alpha view over automation vessels, brewery equipment, and timed brew-session steps.
crp.alphaNote: Rows marked as projected are read models. Automation and brewery remain the sources of truth; no CRP rows are created from this page.
crp.error: Could not load capacity planning data.
crp.noResources: No capacity resources or automation projections in this workspace yet.
crp.noWorkCenters: No work-center projections in this workspace yet.
crp.noCapacity: No capacity load buckets are visible yet.
crp.noSchedule: No scheduled operations are visible yet.

resources.workCentersNote: Work centers are read-only brewery equipment profile projections. They explain the capacity context without moving ownership out of brewery.

capacity.title: Capacity load
capacity.note: Alpha buckets with 0 available minutes are read-model evidence, not final CRP calendar capacity.

ads.contactLine: Contact us for hosting your ad here.
ads.upgradeLine: Upgrade to a paid plan for removal.

platformAds.title: Platform ads
platformAds.subtitle: Manage platform-scoped ads (web). Visible across accounts unless ads are disabled for a workspace.
platformAds.hint: Tip: preview placements by opening any page and hard-reloading (locale: {locale}).
platformAds.globalBottomNote: Note: the Global bottom placement is hidden on the recipe edit page to avoid layout jumps on mobile.
platformAds.notAuthorized: You are not authorized to manage platform ads.

platformRecipes.title: Platform recipes import/export
platformRecipes.subtitle: Import or export recipes for any workspace. Select a workspace first.
platformRecipes.notAuthorized: You are not authorized to manage platform recipes.
platformRecipes.workspaceRequired: Select a workspace to import or export.
platformRecipes.accountRequired: Select a workspace to import or export.
platformRecipes.exportFullNote: Platform export includes full BeerJSON and recipeExtJson.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.subtitle: Adjust contrast, text size, and spacing. Saved on this device; if you are signed in, settings also sync to your account.
accessibility.note: Tip: your browser zoom also works, and respects locale ({locale}).

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Desktop-first web app + native mobile apps, offline-first brew-day logging.

importExport.title: Import / Export
importExport.supportedNote: Recipe import/export interoperability is centered on BeerJSON (canonical).
importExport.customImportCta: Contact us for affordable custom import.

fermDataIntegration.title: Ferm Data & Integration
fermDataIntegration.subtitle: Integrations and fermentation data (stats and insights).

integration.title: Floating hydrometers integrations
integration.intro: Each integration has its own Cloud URL. Pick your device type and follow its steps.
integration.tiltSubtitle: Use this if you have a Tilt gateway or TiltPi.
integration.step2: 2) Paste the Cloud URL into TiltPi or the Tilt app
integration.step3: 3) After the first log, attach the device to a brew session.
integration.ispindelSubtitle: Token management will be available soon.
integration.raptSubtitle: Token management will be available soon.
integration.cloudUrlAriaTilt: Cloud URL (copy into your Tilt gateway)
integration.cloudUrlHelp: Use this URL in your gateway.
integration.cloudUrlHelpTilt: Use this URL in TiltPi / Tilt 2 “Custom Cloud URL”.
integration.cloudUrlHelpGeneric: Use this URL in your device gateway once supported.
integration.tokenHelp: This token is shown once. Save it if you need to reconfigure; otherwise rotate a new token later.
integration.noDevices: No Tilt devices have logged yet.

brewdayStepsSettings.title: Brewday Steps Settings
brewdayStepsSettings.subtitle: Configure brew day steps, timers, and brewing type.
brewdayStepsSettings.defaultSectionNote: Brew steps: add to your default setup. You can exclude them in brew day if you use only for specific recipes but you can not add in brew day workflow if not added here.
brewdayStepsSettings.customSectionNote: Add custom steps with name, optional minutes, and assigned section.
brewdayStepsSettings.name: Name

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: For custom import requests, send us a message and we’ll reply with next steps and an estimate.

appPermissions.title: App permissions
appPermissions.subtitle: User = your login identity. Workspace = shared tenant boundary. Roles (brewery_admin/member/viewer) and ACL determine what you can do.

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.subtitle: Help improve translations and brewing datasets used by the app.

rawMaterials.title: Help improve raw materials database
rawMaterials.subtitle: If you find missing or incorrect fermentables/hops/yeast/other ingredients (or water-related profiles), tell us so we can improve the canonical dataset.
rawMaterials.step1: Note what’s missing/incorrect (name, producer, key specs like color/yield/AA% where applicable).
rawMaterials.step2: Include a screenshot or the exact values if possible (helps avoid ambiguity).
rawMaterials.step3: Open a report using our Raw materials issue template (coming soon).
rawMaterials.issueTemplateNote: We’ll add a direct link once the GitHub contribution workflow is finalized.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Brewery App is an internal tool for recipe development and brew-day logging, with a water chemistry calculator inspired by BrunWater, Troester and others. Beer.json schema fully supported. Raw material database of choice for v0 is BeerProto.
about.translationsRowPrefix: Translations are welcome and managed via our contribution workflow (see
about.translationsSideNote: Side note: this is just a primer — the translation contribution workflow is planned, but not fully in place yet.

selectAccount.title: Select workspace
selectAccount.subtitle: Choose which workspace you want to work in.

selectWorkspace.title: Select workspace
selectWorkspace.subtitle: Choose which workspace you want to work in.

auth.noteBody: English is the original language. Other languages (starting with Italian) will soon be 100% supported. Spanish and German are coming soon as well.

sessionExpired.title: Session expired
sessionExpired.body: You have been signed out. Redirecting you to sign in again.

i18nContributing.title: Help translate (i18n contributing)
i18nContributing.subtitle: We welcome translation contributions. English is the source of truth; Italian is the first community language.
i18nContributing.howItWorks1: The web app uses locale-prefixed URLs (e.g. /en/..., /it/...).
i18nContributing.howItWorks3: Keys should be stable. Don’t change keys unless you’re intentionally refactoring text across the app.
i18nContributing.recommendedToolBody: We recommend using Weblate (web UI) connected to GitHub. It allows anyone to translate without touching git, and it opens PRs automatically.
i18nContributing.recommendedTool1: Translate using a browser (no git required).
i18nContributing.recommendedTool2: Maintainers review and merge Weblate PRs.
i18nContributing.githubFallbackBody: If you’re comfortable with git/GitHub, you can also open a PR editing the JSON message files directly.
i18nContributing.rule2: Keep meaning accurate; avoid overly literal translations if they read unnaturally.
i18nContributing.rule3: Don’t translate technical identifiers like file paths, API paths, or code.

ai.title: AI Consultant
ai.subtitle: Ask questions about your workspace — recipes, products, vessels, production planning, and inventory.

composer.placeholder: Ask a question about your brewery…

messages.empty: Start a conversation by asking a question below.

errors.subscriptionRequired: AI consultant is available on paid tiers.
errors.notEnabled: AI consultant is not enabled in this workspace. Ask an admin to enable it.
errors.noKey: No AI provider key configured. An admin must add one in the AI settings.
errors.dataEgressNotAccepted: An admin must accept the data-egress notice before AI calls can be made.
errors.rateLimit: AI usage limit reached. Try again later.
errors.rateLimitRole: Your role’s monthly AI usage limit has been reached.
errors.rateLimitUserDaily: Your daily AI usage limit has been reached.
errors.internal: Something went wrong. Please try again.

settings.title: AI Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure the AI consultant for this workspace. Admin-only.
settings.memberOnlyNotice: Only workspace admins can change AI settings. You can still use the consultant once it has been enabled.
settings.enableHint: When enabled, members can use the AI consultant in this workspace.
settings.providerHint: Anthropic (full tools) or OpenAI BYOK (chat without tools until adapter completes).
settings.apiKeyHint: Stored encrypted at rest. We never display the key back; clear by saving an empty value.
settings.dataEgressLabel: I understand that messages and tool results will be sent to Anthropic for processing.
settings.dataEgressHint: Required by EU + US privacy regimes. Tool results may include recipe, equipment, and inventory data from this workspace.
settings.roleLimitsHint: Sum of input + output tokens over the trailing 30 days. 0 = no cap.
settings.perUserDailyCapHint: Sum of input + output tokens today (UTC). 0 = no cap.

concierge.title: Need help setting up?
concierge.body: Book a 15-minute call with our team. Italian and English available.

upgrade.title: Unlock the AI consultant
upgrade.body: The AI consultant is included with the paid plan. Upgrade your workspace to ask questions in plain language about your recipes, equipment, brew sessions, and inventory — answered using your workspace’s real data.
upgrade.bullet1: Read-only access to your workspace data

concierge.title: Want help getting set up?
concierge.body: Once your workspace is upgraded, book a 15-minute call with our team. Italian and English available.

usage.title: AI usage
usage.description: Aggregated token spend, per-user activity, and role limits for this workspace.

chart.title: Token spend — last 30 days
chart.ariaLabel: Bar chart of daily token spend over the last 30 days

alerts.heading: Limits approaching
alerts.roleApproachingLimit: Role '{role}' is at {percent} of its monthly cap ({used}/{limit}).
alerts.userApproachingDailyCap: User '{user}' is at {percent} of today's cap ({used}/{cap}).

demo.summaryLine: Public demonstration environment on demo.umbraculum.dev — illustrative data only; may be reset without notice.
demo.dataLossWarning: Do not store records you cannot afford to lose.
demo.platformIntro: Umbraculum is an open-source platform for workspace-shaped operational applications — shared backbone (auth, workspaces, billing, AI, rendering) plus canonical modules (PIM, MRP, CRP, automation, …) that vertical products compose.
demo.referenceVertical: This demo runs the brewery reference vertical: an example manufacturing product the Umbraculum core team ships in the monorepo to show how a team builds a specific vertical on the platform. It is a showcase, not proof that Umbraculum is brewery-only.
demo.linkBuildingVerticalDesc: how ISVs/integrators build their own product on Umbraculum
demo.unsurePrefix: Unsure? Read the docs above, then ask on the
demo.nativeIntro: Mobile (brewery native): Internal EAS preview builds point at this same origin (https://demo.umbraculum.dev). Sign in with the same demo accounts above. Brew-day flows run on device; MRP, CRP, PIM, and inventory use Open on web (browser on this host).
demo.nativeBulletApk: Build/install APK: repo path apps/native/EAS-DEMO-SETUP.md (GitHub: umbraculum-dev monorepo) or workflow native-eas-build with profile preview.
